evidence: CollocateProfileComparator (package-private class) declares compareCollocateProfiles as public, while its sibling MultiSeedExplorer (also package-private) has no public methods — public on a package-private class is meaningless but inconsistent

ExploreResponseAssembler (final package-private class) declares all ~12 methods as public, while HttpApiUtils (also final package-private) uses a deliberate mix of public and package-private based on intended scope
All other production classes use SLF4J exclusively; Main.java mixes SLF4J with System.out.println/System.err.println for user-facing CLI output

strengths: All api-package handler classes (SketchHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, VisualizationHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers) are uniformly package-private with consistent instance-method handler patterns
Logging is uniform across all non-Main classes: private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(X.class) via SLF4J
Test assertion style is consistent throughout: JUnit 5 Assertions.* via static import across all test classes
Error handling at the HTTP layer is consolidated through wrapWithErrorHandling in HttpApiUtils, applied uniformly across all route registrations
Utils package classes are all public final with private constructor — CqlUtils, LogDiceUtils, MathUtils, RelationPatternUtils, RelationUtils all follow the same pattern
convention_outlier.issue_character: Isolated access-modifier inconsistencies: two package-private classes have public methods where the public keyword is meaningless (CollocateProfileComparator) or over-broad relative to a deliberate peer (ExploreResponseAssembler); the outliers are all local, non-structural, and do not affect runtime behavior.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase demonstrates strong convention discipline: logging, error handling, handler visibility, test assertions, and utility class structure are all consistent. The outliers found are minor access-modifier mismatches in two package-private classes (CollocateProfileComparator and ExploreResponseAssembler) and Main.java's intentional mix of stdout for user output alongside SLF4J for internal logging — which is arguably correct for a CLI entry point. These are not structural problems and affect only two files. The overall picture is a healthy codebase with narrow, low-impact convention deviations, placing the score solidly in the upper-solid range.

issues.summary: CollocateProfileComparator.compareCollocateProfiles is public in a package-private class, inconsistent with sibling MultiSeedExplorer which has no public methods
evidence: CollocateProfileComparator class has no access modifier (package-private) but its primary method is declared public: 'public ComparisonResult compareCollocateProfiles(...)'
MultiSeedExplorer (same package, same visibility, same role) uses package-private method visibility '@NonNull ExplorationResult findCollocateIntersection(...)' — no public keyword
public visibility on a package-private class method is meaningless but signals the outlier intent vs neighbor convention
issues.suggestion: Remove the public modifier from compareCollocateProfiles to align with MultiSeedExplorer's package-private convention: 'ComparisonResult compareCollocateProfiles(...)'

issues.summary: ExploreResponseAssembler declares all methods public in a package-private class, while HttpApiUtils (same role, same package) uses deliberate public/package-private split
evidence: ExploreResponseAssembler is 'final class' (package-private) but all 12 methods are 'public static'

HttpApiUtils is also 'final class' (package-private) but uses a deliberate mix: sendJsonResponse/sendError/parseQueryParams are public while parseIntParam/parseDoubleParam/readBodyWithSizeLimit/wrapWithErrorHandling are package-private
ExploreResponseAssembler methods are used only within the api package and its test package — no cross-package callers exist
issues.suggestion: Align ExploreResponseAssembler with HttpApiUtils's approach: methods used only within the package should be package-private (remove 'public' modifier). Methods exercised directly from test classes in the same package can stay package-private since test classes share the package.

root_causes: Incremental extraction of CollocateProfileComparator from SemanticFieldExplorer likely carried over a public modifier without adjusting for the new package-private class context
ExploreResponseAssembler may have been drafted as a candidate for eventual public API extraction, leaving public modifiers in place after the decision to keep it package-private
likely_symptoms: Developers reading CollocateProfileComparator may assume the public method is intentionally exposed and mimic the pattern in new package-private classes
Minor confusion when comparing ExploreResponseAssembler and HttpApiUtils method visibility — the inconsistency provides no design signal
possible_false_positives: Main.java mixing System.out with SLF4J is intentional: stdout is the correct channel for user-facing CLI progress output, logger for internal diagnostic messages — this is not a real outlier
ExplorationResult and ComparisonResult being classes while other model types are records is intentional and documented in each class's Javadoc
